figures.caption: Table 1: Main results of AUROC and F1-Score compared with different competitive baselines over diverse LLMs on TruthfulQA, TriviaQA, SciQ and HaluEval datasets. The best results are highlighted in bold.

Figure 5: Cross-dataset generalization analysis for CausalGaze and HaluGNN. Each subplot displays the F1-Score when the model is trained on the row dataset and tested on the column dataset, with values annotated in each cell.

Figure 2: Overall framework of the proposed CausalGaze. We first employ the gradient-guided counterfactual intervention to obtain the refined edges, then the hidden states and the refined edges are together passed to the GNN-based detection module for the final hallucination detection result.
Table 4: The contribution of the Projection Layer and its dimentions on Qwen2-7B and Mistral-7B across different datasets. The best results are highlighted in bold.
Table 5: The impact of causal graphs from different layers of Qwen2-7B and Mistral-7B on the detection performance.
Table 2: The contribution of the Projection Layer and its dimentions on Llama2-7B across different datasets. The best results are highlighted in bold.
